title: Lithocarpus synbalanos
taxonomy: Lithocarpus synbalanos is classified as a species of tree within the genus Lithocarpus, which is a part of the beech family Fagaceae. This genus is native to Asia and consists of several species of evergreen trees. Lithocarpus synbalanos is specifically native to the Himalayas and parts of China. The species is recognized for its distinctive leaves and habit of growing in mountainous regions. As part of the Fagaceae family, Lithocarpus synbalanos is closely related to other species of beech and oak trees.
uses: Lithocarpus synbalanos has been found to have various traditional medicinal uses, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is used to treat fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and bark are often used in herbal remedies to alleviate symptoms. Some studies suggest that the plant may have antimicrobial properties, which could help combat infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential. Traditional healers in the region have long valued Lithocarpus synbalanos for its purported health benefits.
